123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

641	    def normalize_reasoning_inputs(cls, values: Dict):
642	        r = values.get("reasoning")
643	        if r is None:
644	            return values
645	
646	        if isinstance(r, dict):
647	            effort = r.get("effort") or r.get("reasoning_effort")
648	            if effort in {"low", "medium", "high"}:
649	                values["reasoning_effort"] = effort
650	
651	            enabled = (
652	                r.get("enabled")
653	                if r.get("enabled") is not None
654	                else r.get("enable", False)
655	            )
656	            if isinstance(enabled, str):
657	                enabled = enabled.strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "y", "on"}
658	            if enabled:
659	                ctk = values.get("chat_template_kwargs")
660	                if not isinstance(ctk, dict):
661	                    ctk = {}
662	                ctk.setdefault("thinking", True)
663	                values["chat_template_kwargs"] = ctk

669	    def set_json_schema(cls, values):
670	        response_format = values.get("response_format")
671	        if not response_format:
672	            return values
673	
674	        if response_format.get("type") != "json_schema":
675	            return values
676	
677	        schema = response_format.pop("schema", None)
678	        json_schema = response_format.get("json_schema")
679	
680	        if json_schema:
681	            return values
682	
683	        if schema:
684	            name_ = schema.get("title", "Schema")
685	            strict_ = False
686	            if "properties" in schema and "strict" in schema["properties"]:
687	                item = schema["properties"].pop("strict", None)
688	                if item and item.get("default", False):
689	                    strict_ = True
690	
691	            response_format["json_schema"] = {
692	                "name": name_,
693	                "schema": schema,
694	                "strict": strict_,
695	            }
696	
697	        return values

699	    def to_sampling_params(
700	        self,
701	        stop: List[str],
702	        model_generation_config: Dict[str, Any],
703	        tool_call_constraint: Optional[ToolCallConstraint] = None,
704	    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
705	        """
706	        Convert request to sampling parameters.
707	        Priority: user value > model generation_config > OpenAI defaults
708	        """
709	
710	        def get_param(param_name: str):
711	            value = getattr(self, param_name)
712	            if value is None:
713	                return model_generation_config.get(
714	                    param_name, self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS[param_name]
715	                )
716	            return value
717	
718	        # add per user request
719	        spaces_between_special_tokens = (
720	            True
721	            if self.chat_template_kwargs is None
722	            else self.chat_template_kwargs.get("spaces_between_special_tokens", True)
723	        )
724	
725	        sampling_params = {
726	            "temperature": get_param("temperature"),
727	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_completion_tokens or self.max_tokens,
728	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_tokens,
729	            "stop": stop,
730	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
731	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
732	            "top_p": get_param("top_p"),
733	            "top_k": get_param("top_k"),
734	            "min_p": get_param("min_p"),
735	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
736	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
737	            "repetition_penalty": get_param("repetition_penalty"),
738	            "regex": self.regex,
739	            "ebnf": self.ebnf,
740	            "n": self.n,
741	            "no_stop_trim": self.no_stop_trim,
742	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
743	            "skip_special_tokens": self.skip_special_tokens,
744	            "logit_bias": self.logit_bias,
745	            "custom_params": self.custom_params,
746	            "sampling_seed": self.seed,
747	            "spaces_between_special_tokens": spaces_between_special_tokens,
748	        }
749	
750	        if self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_schema":
751	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
752	                self.response_format.json_schema.schema_
753	            )
754	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_object":
755	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
756	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "structural_tag":
757	            sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
758	                self.response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
759	            )
760	
761	        # Check if there are already existing output constraints
762	        has_existing_constraints = (
763	            sampling_params.get("regex")
764	            or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
765	            or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
766	            or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
767	        )
768	
769	        if tool_call_constraint and has_existing_constraints:
770	            logger.warning("Constrained decoding is not compatible with tool calls.")
771	        elif tool_call_constraint:
772	            constraint_type, constraint_value = tool_call_constraint
773	            if constraint_type == "structural_tag":
774	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
775	                    constraint_value.model_dump(by_alias=True)
776	                )
777	            elif constraint_type == "json_schema":
778	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
779	                    constraint_value  # type: ignore
780	                )
781	            else:
782	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = constraint_value
783	
784	        return sampling_params

1171	        self, default_max_tokens: int, default_params: Optional[Dict] = None
1172	    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
1173	        """Convert to sampling parameters for generation."""
1174	        if default_params is None:
1175	            default_params = {}
1176	
1177	        # Use max_output_tokens if available, otherwise use max_tokens for backwards compatibility
1178	        if self.max_output_tokens is not None:
1179	            max_tokens = min(self.max_output_tokens, default_max_tokens)
1180	        else:
1181	            max_tokens = default_max_tokens
1182	
1183	        # Avoid exceed the context length by minus 2 token
1184	        max_tokens -= 2
1185	
1186	        # Get parameters with defaults
1187	        temperature = self.temperature
1188	        if temperature is None:
1189	            temperature = default_params.get(
1190	                "temperature", self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"]
1191	            )
1192	
1193	        top_p = self.top_p
1194	        if top_p is None:
1195	            top_p = default_params.get("top_p", self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])
1196	
1197	        params = {
1198	            "max_new_tokens": max_tokens,
1199	            "temperature": temperature,
1200	            "top_p": top_p,
1201	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
1202	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
1203	            "stop": self.stop,
1204	            "top_k": self.top_k,
1205	            "min_p": self.min_p,
1206	            "repetition_penalty": self.repetition_penalty,
1207	        }
1208	
1209	        # Apply any additional default parameters
1210	        for key, value in default_params.items():
1211	            if key not in params or params[key] is None:
1212	                params[key] = value
1213	
1214	        return params

content.text:
I have now completed a thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase. Here is the detailed implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: `json_path` Output Constraint for SGLang

### Architecture Overview

The `json_path` constraint is a **syntactic sugar** layer that converts a JSONPath expression into a JSON Schema, then delegates to the existing `dispatch_json()` grammar backends. This means zero changes to xgrammar, outlines, or llguidance backends. The conversion happens in `GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()` before the grammar key is looked up in the cache.

### Step 1: Create the JSONPath-to-JSON-Schema Converter

**New file**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py`

This module contains a single public function `jsonpath_to_json_schema(expr: str) -> dict` plus a simple recursive parser.

**JSONPath Grammar to Support**:
- `$` - root (required prefix)
- `.property` - child property access
- `[*]` - all array items (wildcard)
- `[N]` - specific array index (translates to `minItems: N+1`)

**Parsing Algorithm**:
1. Validate the expression starts with `$`
2. Tokenize the remainder into segments: each segment is either `.propertyName` or `[*]` or `[N]`
3. Build the JSON Schema tree from outside-in (root to leaf)

**Conversion rules for each segment type**:

| JSONPath Segment | JSON Schema Fragment |
|---|---|
| `.property` | `{"type": "object", "properties": {"property": <inner>}, "required": ["property"]}` |
| `[*]` | `{"type": "array", "items": <inner>}` |
| `[N]` | `{"type": "array", "minItems": N+1, "items": <inner>}` |
| (terminal leaf) | `{}` (any value) |

**Example conversions**:
- `$.name` becomes: `{"type": "object", "properties": {"name": {}}, "required": ["name"]}`
- `$.store.book[*].author` becomes:
  ```json
  {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "store": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "book": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "author": {}
              },
              "required": ["author"]
            }
          }
        },
        "required": ["book"]
      }
    },
    "required": ["store"]
  }
  ```

**Implementation detail**: The tokenizer should use a simple regex or character-by-character scan. The segments list is built first, then the schema is constructed by folding right-to-left (starting from the leaf `{}` schema and wrapping each segment around it).

```python
import re
import json
from typing import List, Tuple

def jsonpath_to_json_schema(jsonpath_expr: str) -> str:
    """Convert a JSONPath expression to a JSON schema string."""
    segments = _parse_jsonpath(jsonpath_expr)
    schema = _build_schema(segments)
    return json.dumps(schema)

def _parse_jsonpath(expr: str) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
    """Parse JSONPath into a list of (type, value) segments."""
    if not expr.startswith("$"):
        raise ValueError(f"JSONPath must start with '$', got: {expr}")
    
    rest = expr[1:]  # strip the '$'
    segments = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(rest):
        if rest[i] == '.':
            # Property access: .propertyName
            match = re.match(r'\.([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)', rest[i:])
            if not match:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid property at position {i}: {rest[i:]}")
            segments.append(("property", match.group(1)))
            i += match.end()
        elif rest[i] == '[':
            # Array access: [*] or [N]
            match = re.match(r'\[(\*|\d+)\]', rest[i:])
            if not match:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid array access at position {i}: {rest[i:]}")
            index_str = match.group(1)
            if index_str == '*':
                segments.append(("wildcard", "*"))
            else:
                segments.append(("index", index_str))
            i += match.end()
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unexpected character at position {i}: {rest[i]}")
    
    if not segments:
        raise ValueError("JSONPath must specify at least one path segment after '$'")
    
    return segments

def _build_schema(segments: List[Tuple[str, str]]) -> dict:
    """Build a JSON schema from parsed segments, folding right-to-left."""
    schema = {}  # leaf: any value
    for seg_type, seg_value in reversed(segments):
        if seg_type == "property":
            schema = {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {seg_value: schema},
                "required": [seg_value],
            }
        elif seg_type == "wildcard":
            schema = {
                "type": "array",
                "items": schema,
            }
        elif seg_type == "index":
            schema = {
                "type": "array",
                "minItems": int(seg_value) + 1,
                "items": schema,
            }
    return schema
```

**Validation**: The function should raise `ValueError` with a clear message for:
- Missing `$` prefix
- Empty path (just `$`)
- Malformed property names (e.g., `.123invalid`)
- Unclosed brackets
- Unsupported syntax (recursive descent `..`, filter expressions `[?(...)]`, etc.)

### Step 2: Add `json_path` to Server-Side `SamplingParams`

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

**Changes**:

1. Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter to `SamplingParams.__init__()` (after `structural_tag` at line 58, to keep constraint params grouped together).

2. Add `self.json_path = json_path` assignment in the constructor body (after `self.structural_tag = structural_tag` at line 87).

3. Update the `verify()` method's mutual exclusivity check (lines 155-161). Change:
   ```python
   grammars = [
       self.json_schema,
       self.regex,
       self.ebnf,
   ]
   ```
   to:
   ```python
   grammars = [
       self.json_schema,
       self.regex,
       self.ebnf,
       self.json_path,
   ]
   ```
   And update the error message to include `json_path`:
   ```python
   if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
       raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, ebnf, or json_path can be set.")
   ```

**Note**: `structural_tag` is deliberately excluded from the mutual exclusivity list in the current code (it is handled separately by tool call logic). `json_path` should be included alongside the other user-facing grammar constraints.

### Step 3: Handle `json_path` in Grammar Manager

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

**Changes** to `process_req_with_grammar()`:

1. Add `json_path` to the existence check at line 71-75. Add `or req.sampling_params.json_path is not None` to the condition.

2. Add `json_path` to the error message at line 77.

3. Add a new branch for `json_path` in the key-building block (lines 80-87). Crucially, the `json_path` is converted to a JSON schema and then uses the `("json", ...)` cache key. This means that identical JSON schemas produced from different JSONPath expressions (or from a direct `json_schema` param) will share the same cache entry.

   ```python
   elif req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
       from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_constraint import jsonpath_to_json_schema
       try:
           json_schema_str = jsonpath_to_json_schema(req.sampling_params.json_path)
       except ValueError as e:
           req.set_finish_with_abort(f"Invalid json_path expression: {e}")
           return False
       key = ("json", json_schema_str)
   ```

   This must be placed before the existing `elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:` branch to maintain the cascade priority.

**Key design decision**: By converting to JSON schema and using the `("json", ...)` cache key type, we reuse the existing `dispatch_json()` path in all grammar backends. No changes to `base_grammar_backend.py`, `xgrammar_backend.py`, `outlines_backend.py`, or `llguidance_backend.py` are needed.

### Step 4: Add `json_path` to the Frontend API

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/lang/api.py`

**Changes**:

1. Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter to the `gen()` function signature (after `json_schema` at line 98).

2. Pass `json_path` to the `SglGen` constructor call (after `json_schema` at line 138).

### Step 5: Add `json_path` to the IR Layer

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

**Changes to `SglSamplingParams`**:

1. Add field `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to the dataclass (after `json_schema` at line 36).

2. In `clone()` (line 42-62): The `clone()` method currently uses positional arguments which is fragile. Add `self.json_path` to the clone -- but note this method does NOT currently copy `dtype` or `regex` (they are separate fields). Since `json_path` is a constraint like `json_schema` and `regex`, it should follow the same pattern. The `json_schema` field IS cloned (line 61), but `dtype` and `regex` are not. So `json_path` should be added to the cloned fields just after `json_schema`. However, since `clone()` uses positional args matching the dataclass field order, the simplest approach is to NOT add it to clone() directly but instead add it as a separate field like `dtype` and `regex` (which are not included in `clone()`'s positional list). Looking more carefully, `json_path` behaves like `json_schema`: it is passed via `to_srt_kwargs()` and should be cloned. The `clone()` method does NOT clone `dtype` or `regex`. But it DOES clone `json_schema`. The safest path: add `json_path` as a dataclass field after `json_schema`, and do NOT add it to the positional `clone()` call (to avoid breaking the positional signature). Instead, ensure `json_path` is handled in `to_srt_kwargs()`. The `clone()` omission is consistent with how `dtype` and `regex` are handled.

3. In `to_srt_kwargs()` (lines 121-138): Add `"json_path": self.json_path` to the returned dict.

**Changes to `SglGen.__init__`**:

1. Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter (after `json_schema` at line 474).

2. Pass `json_path=json_path` to the `SglSamplingParams` constructor (after `json_schema=json_schema` at line 499).

### Step 6: Add `json_path` to the Interpreter

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`

**Change at line 809**: Add `"json_path"` to the list of fields that are copied from `sampling_params` to the clone. It should be added after `"json_schema"`.

### Step 7: Add `json_path` to the OpenAI-Compatible Protocol

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

**Changes to `CompletionRequest`** (around line 268):
1. Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` after `ebnf` (line 270).

**Changes to `ChatCompletionRequest`** (around line 580):
1. Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` after `ebnf` (line 581).

**Changes to `ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()`** (around line 738):
1. Add `"json_path": self.json_path` to the `sampling_params` dict (after the `"ebnf"` entry at line 739).

2. Add `json_path` to the `has_existing_constraints` check at lines 762-766:
   ```python
   has_existing_constraints = (
       sampling_params.get("regex")
       or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
       or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
       or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
       or sampling_params.get("json_path")
   )
   ```

### Step 8: Add `json_path` to Completions Serving

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py`

**Changes** around line 144:
1. Add `"json_path": request.json_path` to the `sampling_params` dict (after `"ebnf": request.ebnf`).

### Step 9: Add `json_path` to gRPC Server (Optional but Recommended)

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/grpc_server.py`

Add a `json_path` field to the gRPC proto and handler. This follows the same pattern as `json_schema` at lines 635-646 and 686.

### Step 10: Write Tests

**New test file**: `test/srt/constrained/test_json_path_constraint.py`

Unit tests for the converter function:

```python
class TestJsonPathToJsonSchema(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_simple_property(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.name"))
        assert schema == {"type": "object", "properties": {"name": {}}, "required": ["name"]}
    
    def test_nested_properties(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.a.b.c"))
        assert schema["type"] == "object"
        assert "a" in schema["properties"]
        inner = schema["properties"]["a"]
        assert inner["properties"]["b"]["properties"]["c"] == {}
    
    def test_array_wildcard(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.items[*]"))
        assert schema["properties"]["items"]["type"] == "array"
    
    def test_array_with_property(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.store.book[*].author"))
        book = schema["properties"]["store"]["properties"]["book"]
        assert book["type"] == "array"
        assert book["items"]["properties"]["author"] == {}
    
    def test_array_index(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.data[0]"))
        assert schema["properties"]["data"]["minItems"] == 1
    
    def test_invalid_no_dollar(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            jsonpath_to_json_schema("name")
    
    def test_invalid_empty_path(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            jsonpath_to_json_schema("$")
    
    def test_invalid_filter_expression(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.store.book[?(@.price < 10)]")
```

An integration test should also be added (requires a running model server, similar to `test/registered/openai_server/features/test_json_mode.py`) that sends a request with `json_path="$.name"` and validates the output is valid JSON with a `name` field.

### Dependency Graph / Sequencing

```
Step 1 (json_path_constraint.py) - no dependencies, can be built first
    |
    v
Step 2 (SamplingParams) - no dependencies on Step 1
    |
    v
Step 3 (GrammarManager) - depends on Steps 1 and 2
    |
    v
Steps 4-6 (Frontend API, IR, Interpreter) - independent of Steps 2-3, can be parallel
    |
    v
Steps 7-8 (Protocol, Serving) - independent of Steps 4-6, can be parallel
    |
    v
Step 10 (Tests) - depends on all above
```

Steps 1, 2, and 4-6 can all be developed in parallel. Step 3 ties them together. Steps 7-8 can also be developed in parallel with 4-6.

### Potential Challenges and Edge Cases

1. **Cache key collisions**: Two different JSONPath expressions can produce the same JSON schema (e.g., there are no such cases with the subset we support, but if we add `..` recursive descent in the future). This is actually beneficial: it maximizes cache reuse.

2. **Error handling in grammar manager**: If `jsonpath_to_json_schema()` raises `ValueError`, the request should be aborted with a clear error message. This is handled in Step 3 with a try/except.

3. **The `clone()` method in `SglSamplingParams`**: This uses positional arguments matching dataclass field order. Adding `json_path` as a new field changes the positional signature. However, `clone()` only copies up to `json_schema` (the 18th field) and does not copy `dtype` or `regex`. So `json_path` should be placed AFTER `json_schema` but its omission from `clone()` is fine since the constraint will be carried via `to_srt_kwargs()`.

4. **Mutual exclusivity**: `json_path` is mutually exclusive with `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf`. If a user specifies both `json_path` and `json_schema`, the `verify()` method will raise an error.

5. **JSONPath features NOT supported**: Recursive descent (`..`), filter expressions (`[?(...)]`), union (`[a,b]`), and slice (`[start:end]`). These should raise clear `ValueError` messages pointing out the limitation.

6. **Property names with special characters**: The regex `[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*` restricts property names to identifier-like strings. JSON keys can contain any characters, but for the initial implementation this restriction is reasonable and covers the vast majority of use cases.

### Files Summary

| File | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py` | **CREATE** | JSONPath parser and JSON Schema converter |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | MODIFY | Add `json_path` param, mutual exclusivity |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | MODIFY | Convert `json_path` to JSON schema, use `("json", ...)` cache key |
| `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | MODIFY | Add `json_path` param to `gen()` |
| `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | MODIFY | Add `json_path` to `SglSamplingParams`, `SglGen`, and `to_srt_kwargs()` |
| `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` | MODIFY | Add `"json_path"` to field copy list |
| `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | MODIFY | Add `json_path` to `CompletionRequest`, `ChatCompletionRequest`, and `to_sampling_params()` |
| `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py` | MODIFY | Add `json_path` to sampling_params dict |
| `test/srt/constrained/test_json_path_constraint.py` | **CREATE** | Unit tests for the converter |

### Critical Files for Implementation

I need to design an implementation plan for adding a new "json_path" output constraint to SGLang. This constraint validates that generated output is valid JSON containing a specified JSONPath pattern (e.g., "$.store.book[*].author").

## Context: How SGLang constraints work

SGLang has a multi-layered constraint system:

### Layer 1: Frontend API (`python/sglang/lang/api.py`)
- `gen()` function accepts constraint params like `regex=`, `json_schema=`
- Creates `SglGen` IR node (in `python/sglang/lang/ir.py`)
- `SglGen` stores constraints in `SglSamplingParams` dataclass

### Layer 2: SglSamplingParams IR (`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`)
- Dataclass with fields: `json_schema`, `regex`, `dtype`
- `to_srt_kwargs()` passes `regex` and `json_schema` to the SRT backend
- Other backends (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) don't support custom constraints

### Layer 3: SamplingParams (`python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`)
- Server-side params: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- `verify()` enforces mutual exclusivity — only ONE grammar constraint allowed
- These are the params used by the runtime engine

### Layer 4: Grammar Manager (`python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`)
- `process_req_with_grammar()` maps constraint params to cache keys:
  - `json_schema` → key `("json", schema_string)`
  - `regex` → key `("regex", pattern_string)`
  - `ebnf` → key `("ebnf", grammar_string)`
  - `structural_tag` → key `("structural_tag", tag_string)`
- Submits compilation to thread pool, returns Future

### Layer 5: Grammar Backend Dispatch (`python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`)
- `_init_value_dispatch()` routes by key type to `dispatch_json()`, `dispatch_regex()`, `dispatch_ebnf()`, `dispatch_structural_tag()`
- Each backend (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance) implements these dispatch methods
- `dispatch_json()` typically converts a JSON schema to a grammar/FSM

### Layer 6: OpenAI API Protocol (`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`)
- Chat/Completion request models have `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf` fields
- `to_sampling_params()` builds the sampling params dict from request fields
- Constraint mutual exclusivity is checked

## Key Design Decision

The "json_path" constraint requires generating JSON that:
1. Is valid JSON
2. Contains at least one value matching a specified JSONPath expression

**The cleanest approach**: Convert a JSONPath expression into a JSON Schema that enforces the required structure. For example:
- `"$.store.book[*].author"` → JSON schema requiring an object with `store.book` array where items have `author` field
- `"$.name"` → JSON schema requiring an object with `name` property

This way the json_path constraint leverages the existing `dispatch_json()` backends (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance) with zero changes to the grammar backends.

**Alternative approach**: Post-validation — generate JSON first, then validate with jsonpath. But this doesn't constrain token-by-token generation, which is the whole point of SGLang constraints.

## Implementation Requirements

1. Add `json_path` parameter to the frontend API (`gen()`, `SglGen`, `SglSamplingParams`)
2. Add `json_path` parameter to `SamplingParams` (server-side)
3. Add `json_path` to the OpenAI protocol request models
4. Add `json_path` to the mutual exclusivity check in `verify()`
5. In `GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()`, handle `json_path` by converting to JSON schema and using the `("json", ...)` cache key
6. Write a utility function `jsonpath_to_json_schema(jsonpath_expr)` that converts a JSONPath to a minimal JSON schema
7. Update `to_sampling_params()` in protocol.py to include `json_path`
8. Add `json_path` to the constraint existence check

## JSONPath → JSON Schema Conversion

The JSONPath syntax we need to support:
- `$.property` → `{"type": "object", "properties": {"property": {}}, "required": ["property"]}`
- `$.a.b.c` → nested objects
- `$.a[*]` → array items
- `$.a[*].b` → array items with property
- `$.a.b[0]` → array with minItems: 1
- `$.store.book[*].author` → nested structure

Design a `jsonpath_to_json_schema()` function that parses the JSONPath expression and builds a JSON schema tree.

## Files to Modify

1. `python/sglang/lang/api.py` — add `json_path` param to `gen()`
2. `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` — add `json_path` to `SglSamplingParams` and `SglGen`
3. `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` — add `json_path` to `SamplingParams`
4. `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` — handle `json_path` conversion
5. `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` — add to API protocol
6. NEW: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py` — conversion utility

Please design a detailed implementation plan covering all these changes, including the JSONPath parser/converter logic.

I have now completed a thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase. Here is the detailed implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: `json_path` Output Constraint for SGLang

### Architecture Overview

The `json_path` constraint is a **syntactic sugar** layer that converts a JSONPath expression into a JSON Schema, then delegates to the existing `dispatch_json()` grammar backends. This means zero changes to xgrammar, outlines, or llguidance backends. The conversion happens in `GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()` before the grammar key is looked up in the cache.

### Step 1: Create the JSONPath-to-JSON-Schema Converter

**New file**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py`

This module contains a single public function `jsonpath_to_json_schema(expr: str) -> dict` plus a simple recursive parser.

**JSONPath Grammar to Support**:
- `$` - root (required prefix)
- `.property` - child property access
- `[*]` - all array items (wildcard)
- `[N]` - specific array index (translates to `minItems: N+1`)

**Parsing Algorithm**:
1. Validate the expression starts with `$`
2. Tokenize the remainder into segments: each segment is either `.propertyName` or `[*]` or `[N]`
3. Build the JSON Schema tree from outside-in (root to leaf)

**Conversion rules for each segment type**:

| JSONPath Segment | JSON Schema Fragment |
|---|---|
| `.property` | `{"type": "object", "properties": {"property": <inner>}, "required": ["property"]}` |
| `[*]` | `{"type": "array", "items": <inner>}` |
| `[N]` | `{"type": "array", "minItems": N+1, "items": <inner>}` |
| (terminal leaf) | `{}` (any value) |

**Example conversions**:
- `$.name` becomes: `{"type": "object", "properties": {"name": {}}, "required": ["name"]}`
- `$.store.book[*].author` becomes:
  ```json
  {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "store": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "book": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "author": {}
              },
              "required": ["author"]
            }
          }
        },
        "required": ["book"]
      }
    },
    "required": ["store"]
  }
  ```

**Implementation detail**: The tokenizer should use a simple regex or character-by-character scan. The segments list is built first, then the schema is constructed by folding right-to-left (starting from the leaf `{}` schema and wrapping each segment around it).

```python
import re
import json
from typing import List, Tuple

def jsonpath_to_json_schema(jsonpath_expr: str) -> str:
    """Convert a JSONPath expression to a JSON schema string."""
    segments = _parse_jsonpath(jsonpath_expr)
    schema = _build_schema(segments)
    return json.dumps(schema)

def _parse_jsonpath(expr: str) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
    """Parse JSONPath into a list of (type, value) segments."""
    if not expr.startswith("$"):
        raise ValueError(f"JSONPath must start with '$', got: {expr}")
    
    rest = expr[1:]  # strip the '$'
    segments = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(rest):
        if rest[i] == '.':
            # Property access: .propertyName
            match = re.match(r'\.([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)', rest[i:])
            if not match:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid property at position {i}: {rest[i:]}")
            segments.append(("property", match.group(1)))
            i += match.end()
        elif rest[i] == '[':
            # Array access: [*] or [N]
            match = re.match(r'\[(\*|\d+)\]', rest[i:])
            if not match:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid array access at position {i}: {rest[i:]}")
            index_str = match.group(1)
            if index_str == '*':
                segments.append(("wildcard", "*"))
            else:
                segments.append(("index", index_str))
            i += match.end()
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unexpected character at position {i}: {rest[i]}")
    
    if not segments:
        raise ValueError("JSONPath must specify at least one path segment after '$'")
    
    return segments

def _build_schema(segments: List[Tuple[str, str]]) -> dict:
    """Build a JSON schema from parsed segments, folding right-to-left."""
    schema = {}  # leaf: any value
    for seg_type, seg_value in reversed(segments):
        if seg_type == "property":
            schema = {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {seg_value: schema},
                "required": [seg_value],
            }
        elif seg_type == "wildcard":
            schema = {
                "type": "array",
                "items": schema,
            }
        elif seg_type == "index":
            schema = {
                "type": "array",
                "minItems": int(seg_value) + 1,
                "items": schema,
            }
    return schema
```

**Validation**: The function should raise `ValueError` with a clear message for:
- Missing `$` prefix
- Empty path (just `$`)
- Malformed property names (e.g., `.123invalid`)
- Unclosed brackets
- Unsupported syntax (recursive descent `..`, filter expressions `[?(...)]`, etc.)

### Step 2: Add `json_path` to Server-Side `SamplingParams`

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

**Note**: `structural_tag` is deliberately excluded from the mutual exclusivity list in the current code (it is handled separately by tool call logic). `json_path` should be included alongside the other user-facing grammar constraints.

### Step 3: Handle `json_path` in Grammar Manager

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503792838-qt6t6r/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

1. Add `json_path` to the existence check at line 71-75. Add `or req.sampling_params.json_path is not None` to the condition.

2. Add `json_path` to the error message at line 77.

3. Add a new branch for `json_path` in the key-building block (lines 80-87). Crucially, the `json_path` is converted to a JSON schema and then uses the `("json", ...)` cache key. This means that identical JSON schemas produced from different JSONPath expressions (or from a direct `json_schema` param) will share the same cache entry.

   ```python
   elif req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
       from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_constraint import jsonpath_to_json_schema
       try:
           json_schema_str = jsonpath_to_json_schema(req.sampling_params.json_path)
       except ValueError as e:
           req.set_finish_with_abort(f"Invalid json_path expression: {e}")
           return False
       key = ("json", json_schema_str)
   ```

   This must be placed before the existing `elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:` branch to maintain the cascade priority.

**Key design decision**: By converting to JSON schema and using the `("json", ...)` cache key type, we reuse the existing `dispatch_json()` path in all grammar backends. No changes to `base_grammar_backend.py`, `xgrammar_backend.py`, `outlines_backend.py`, or `llguidance_backend.py` are needed.

### Step 4: Add `json_path` to the Frontend API

2. In `clone()` (line 42-62): The `clone()` method currently uses positional arguments which is fragile. Add `self.json_path` to the clone -- but note this method does NOT currently copy `dtype` or `regex` (they are separate fields). Since `json_path` is a constraint like `json_schema` and `regex`, it should follow the same pattern. The `json_schema` field IS cloned (line 61), but `dtype` and `regex` are not. So `json_path` should be added to the cloned fields just after `json_schema`. However, since `clone()` uses positional args matching the dataclass field order, the simplest approach is to NOT add it to clone() directly but instead add it as a separate field like `dtype` and `regex` (which are not included in `clone()`'s positional list). Looking more carefully, `json_path` behaves like `json_schema`: it is passed via `to_srt_kwargs()` and should be cloned. The `clone()` method does NOT clone `dtype` or `regex`. But it DOES clone `json_schema`. The safest path: add `json_path` as a dataclass field after `json_schema`, and do NOT add it to the positional `clone()` call (to avoid breaking the positional signature). Instead, ensure `json_path` is handled in `to_srt_kwargs()`. The `clone()` omission is consistent with how `dtype` and `regex` are handled.

### Step 10: Write Tests

**New test file**: `test/srt/constrained/test_json_path_constraint.py`

Unit tests for the converter function:

```python
class TestJsonPathToJsonSchema(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_simple_property(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.name"))
        assert schema == {"type": "object", "properties": {"name": {}}, "required": ["name"]}
    
    def test_nested_properties(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.a.b.c"))
        assert schema["type"] == "object"
        assert "a" in schema["properties"]
        inner = schema["properties"]["a"]
        assert inner["properties"]["b"]["properties"]["c"] == {}
    
    def test_array_wildcard(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.items[*]"))
        assert schema["properties"]["items"]["type"] == "array"
    
    def test_array_with_property(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.store.book[*].author"))
        book = schema["properties"]["store"]["properties"]["book"]
        assert book["type"] == "array"
        assert book["items"]["properties"]["author"] == {}
    
    def test_array_index(self):
        schema = json.loads(jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.data[0]"))
        assert schema["properties"]["data"]["minItems"] == 1
    
    def test_invalid_no_dollar(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            jsonpath_to_json_schema("name")
    
    def test_invalid_empty_path(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            jsonpath_to_json_schema("$")
    
    def test_invalid_filter_expression(self):
        with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
            jsonpath_to_json_schema("$.store.book[?(@.price < 10)]")
```

An integration test should also be added (requires a running model server, similar to `test/registered/openai_server/features/test_json_mode.py`) that sends a request with `json_path="$.name"` and validates the output is valid JSON with a `name` field.

### Dependency Graph / Sequencing

```
Step 1 (json_path_constraint.py) - no dependencies, can be built first
    |
    v
Step 2 (SamplingParams) - no dependencies on Step 1
    |
    v
Step 3 (GrammarManager) - depends on Steps 1 and 2
    |
    v
Steps 4-6 (Frontend API, IR, Interpreter) - independent of Steps 2-3, can be parallel
    |
    v
Steps 7-8 (Protocol, Serving) - independent of Steps 4-6, can be parallel
    |
    v
Step 10 (Tests) - depends on all above
```

Steps 1, 2, and 4-6 can all be developed in parallel. Step 3 ties them together. Steps 7-8 can also be developed in parallel with 4-6.

### Potential Challenges and Edge Cases

1. **Cache key collisions**: Two different JSONPath expressions can produce the same JSON schema (e.g., there are no such cases with the subset we support, but if we add `..` recursive descent in the future). This is actually beneficial: it maximizes cache reuse.

2. **Error handling in grammar manager**: If `jsonpath_to_json_schema()` raises `ValueError`, the request should be aborted with a clear error message. This is handled in Step 3 with a try/except.

3. **The `clone()` method in `SglSamplingParams`**: This uses positional arguments matching dataclass field order. Adding `json_path` as a new field changes the positional signature. However, `clone()` only copies up to `json_schema` (the 18th field) and does not copy `dtype` or `regex`. So `json_path` should be placed AFTER `json_schema` but its omission from `clone()` is fine since the constraint will be carried via `to_srt_kwargs()`.

4. **Mutual exclusivity**: `json_path` is mutually exclusive with `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf`. If a user specifies both `json_path` and `json_schema`, the `verify()` method will raise an error.

5. **JSONPath features NOT supported**: Recursive descent (`..`), filter expressions (`[?(...)]`), union (`[a,b]`), and slice (`[start:end]`). These should raise clear `ValueError` messages pointing out the limitation.

6. **Property names with special characters**: The regex `[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*` restricts property names to identifier-like strings. JSON keys can contain any characters, but for the initial implementation this restriction is reasonable and covers the vast majority of use cases.

| `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py` | MODIFY | Add `json_path` to sampling_params dict |
| `test/srt/constrained/test_json_path_constraint.py` | **CREATE** | Unit tests for the converter |

                request.response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
            )

        return sampling_params

input.content:
# Plan: Add `json_path` Output Constraint to SGLang

## Context

SGLang supports constrained decoding via `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf` parameters. These guide token-by-token generation to produce valid structured output. We need to add a new `json_path` constraint that accepts a JSONPath expression (e.g., `"$.store.book[*].author"`) and ensures the generated JSON contains the specified path structure.

**Design approach**: Convert the JSONPath expression into a JSON Schema at request time, then delegate to the existing `dispatch_json()` grammar backends (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance). This requires **zero changes** to any grammar backend — only the API layers and a new converter utility.

---

## Step 1: Create JSONPath-to-JSON-Schema Converter

**New file**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py`

Create a module with a `jsonpath_to_json_schema(expr: str) -> str` function that:

1. **Parses** the JSONPath expression into segments:
   - `$.prop` → `("property", "prop")`
   - `[*]` → `("wildcard", "*")`
   - `[N]` → `("index", "N")`

2. **Builds** a JSON Schema by folding right-to-left:

   | Segment | Schema Fragment |
   |---------|----------------|
   | `.prop` | `{"type": "object", "properties": {"prop": <inner>}, "required": ["prop"]}` |
   | `[*]` | `{"type": "array", "items": <inner>}` |
   | `[N]` | `{"type": "array", "minItems": N+1, "items": <inner>}` |
   | leaf | `{}` (any value) |

3. **Validates** and rejects unsupported syntax (`..`, `[?()]`, slices) with clear `ValueError` messages.

**Example**: `"$.store.book[*].author"` →
```json
{"type":"object","properties":{"store":{"type":"object","properties":{"book":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"author":{}},"required":["author"]}}},"required":["book"]}},"required":["store"]}
```

---

## Step 2: Add `json_path` to `SamplingParams` (Server-Side)

**File**: `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

- Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter to `__init__()` (after `structural_tag`, line 58)
- Add `self.json_path = json_path` in the constructor body (after line 87)
- Update `verify()` mutual exclusivity check (line 155-161):
  ```python
  grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf, self.json_path]
  ```
  Update error message: `"Only one of regex, json_schema, ebnf, or json_path can be set."`

---

## Step 3: Handle `json_path` in Grammar Manager

**File**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

In `process_req_with_grammar()`:

- Add `or req.sampling_params.json_path is not None` to the existence check (line 71-75)
- Add `json_path` to the error message string (line 77)
- Add new branch before the `structural_tag` branch (after line 85):
  ```python
  elif req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
      from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_constraint import jsonpath_to_json_schema
      try:
          json_schema_str = jsonpath_to_json_schema(req.sampling_params.json_path)
      except ValueError as e:
          req.set_finish_with_abort(f"Invalid json_path expression: {e}")
          return False
      key = ("json", json_schema_str)
  ```
  This converts to JSON schema and reuses the `("json", ...)` cache key, leveraging existing `dispatch_json()` in all backends.

---

## Step 4: Add `json_path` to Frontend API

**File**: `python/sglang/lang/api.py`

- Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter to `gen()` (after `json_schema`, line 98)
- Pass `json_path` to `SglGen()` constructor (after `json_schema`, line 138)

---

## Step 5: Add `json_path` to IR Layer

**File**: `python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

**`SglSamplingParams` (line 17-138)**:
- Add field: `json_path: Optional[str] = None` (after `json_schema`, line 36)
- Add `"json_path": self.json_path` to `to_srt_kwargs()` return dict (after `json_schema`, line 137)
- Note: `clone()` uses positional args matching only up to `json_schema`; `json_path` follows the same pattern as `dtype`/`regex` (not positionally cloned, but carried via kwargs)

**`SglGen.__init__` (line 451-500)**:
- Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter (after `json_schema`, line 474)
- Pass `json_path=json_path` to `SglSamplingParams()` constructor (after `json_schema=json_schema`, line 499)

---

## Step 6: Add `json_path` to Interpreter

**File**: `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`

- Add `"json_path"` to the field copy list at line 809 (after `"json_schema"`)

---

## Step 7: Add `json_path` to OpenAI-Compatible API Protocol

**File**: `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

**`CompletionRequest`** (line 268-270):
- Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` (after `ebnf`, line 270)

**`ChatCompletionRequest`** (line 576-581):
- Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` (after `ebnf`, line 581)

**`ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()`** (line 725-784):
- Add `"json_path": self.json_path` to sampling_params dict (after `"ebnf"`, line 739)
- Add `or sampling_params.get("json_path")` to `has_existing_constraints` check (line 762-766)

---

## Step 8: Add `json_path` to Completions Serving

**File**: `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py`

- Add `"json_path": request.json_path` to sampling_params dict (after `"ebnf"`, line 145)

---

## Step 9: Add `json_path` to Tokenizer Manager Grammar Check

**File**: `python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py`

- Add `or obj.sampling_params.get("json_path", None)` to `_request_has_grammar()` (line 1937-1943)

---

## Step 10: Write Unit Tests

**New file**: `test/srt/constrained/test_json_path_constraint.py`

Test cases for `jsonpath_to_json_schema()`:
- `$.name` → object with required `name` property
- `$.a.b.c` → nested required object properties
- `$.items[*]` → object with array property
- `$.store.book[*].author` → full nested structure with array
- `$.data[0]` → array with `minItems: 1`
- `$.data[2].name` → array with `minItems: 3`, items have `name`
- Error: missing `$` prefix
- Error: empty path (`$` alone)
- Error: unsupported filter `[?(@.price)]`
- Error: unsupported recursive descent `..`
- Error: malformed bracket `[`

---

## Files Summary

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py` | **CREATE** |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | MODIFY |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | MODIFY |
| `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | MODIFY |
| `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | MODIFY |
| `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` | MODIFY |
| `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | MODIFY |
| `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py` | MODIFY |
| `python/sglang/srt/managers/tokenizer_manager.py` | MODIFY |
| `test/srt/constrained/test_json_path_constraint.py` | **CREATE** |

---

## Verification

1. **Unit tests**: Run `python -m pytest test/srt/constrained/test_json_path_constraint.py` to validate the JSONPath→Schema converter
2. **Import check**: `python -c "from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_constraint import jsonpath_to_json_schema; print(jsonpath_to_json_schema('$.store.book[*].author'))"` 
3. **API surface check**: `python -c "from sglang.lang.api import gen; g = gen('test', json_path='$.name'); print(g.sampling_params.json_path)"`
4. **Mutual exclusivity**: Verify `SamplingParams(json_path="$.x", regex=".*").verify(1000)` raises `ValueError`

The plan is complete. Here's a summary:

## Plan Summary

**Approach**: Convert JSONPath expressions to JSON Schemas at request time, then delegate to the existing `dispatch_json()` grammar backends. This means **zero changes** to any grammar backend (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance) — only API layers and a new converter utility.

**10 steps across 10 files** (2 new, 8 modified):

1. **Create** `json_path_constraint.py` — parser that converts JSONPath (e.g. `$.store.book[*].author`) into an equivalent JSON Schema
2. **Modify** `sampling_params.py` — add `json_path` param + mutual exclusivity check
3. **Modify** `grammar_manager.py` — convert `json_path` → JSON schema, reuse `("json", ...)` cache key
4. **Modify** `api.py` — add `json_path` to `gen()` 
5. **Modify** `ir.py` — add to `SglSamplingParams` and `SglGen`
6. **Modify** `interpreter.py` — add to field copy list
7. **Modify** `protocol.py` — add to OpenAI-compatible request models
8. **Modify** `serving_completions.py` — pass through to sampling params
9. **Modify** `tokenizer_manager.py` — add to grammar detection check
10. **Create** unit tests for the converter

The key insight is that JSONPath → JSON Schema conversion happens once per request, and the resulting schema feeds into the existing grammar compilation + caching pipeline unchanged.

# Plan: Add `json_path` Output Constraint to SGLang

## Context

SGLang supports constrained decoding via `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf` parameters. These guide token-by-token generation to produce valid structured output. We need to add a new `json_path` constraint that accepts a JSONPath expression (e.g., `"$.store.book[*].author"`) and ensures the generated JSON contains the specified path structure.

**Design approach**: Convert the JSONPath expression into a JSON Schema at request time, then delegate to the existing `dispatch_json()` grammar backends (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance). This requires **zero changes** to any grammar backend — only the API layers and a new converter utility.

---

## Step 1: Create JSONPath-to-JSON-Schema Converter

**New file**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py`

Create a module with a `jsonpath_to_json_schema(expr: str) -> str` function that:

1. **Parses** the JSONPath expression into segments:
   - `$.prop` → `("property", "prop")`
   - `[*]` → `("wildcard", "*")`
   - `[N]` → `("index", "N")`

2. **Builds** a JSON Schema by folding right-to-left:

   | Segment | Schema Fragment |
   |---------|----------------|
   | `.prop` | `{"type": "object", "properties": {"prop": <inner>}, "required": ["prop"]}` |
   | `[*]` | `{"type": "array", "items": <inner>}` |
   | `[N]` | `{"type": "array", "minItems": N+1, "items": <inner>}` |
   | leaf | `{}` (any value) |

3. **Validates** and rejects unsupported syntax (`..`, `[?()]`, slices) with clear `ValueError` messages.

**Example**: `"$.store.book[*].author"` →
```json
{"type":"object","properties":{"store":{"type":"object","properties":{"book":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"author":{}},"required":["author"]}}},"required":["book"]}},"required":["store"]}
```

---

## Step 2: Add `json_path` to `SamplingParams` (Server-Side)

**File**: `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

- Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter to `__init__()` (after `structural_tag`, line 58)
- Add `self.json_path = json_path` in the constructor body (after line 87)
- Update `verify()` mutual exclusivity check (line 155-161):
  ```python
  grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf, self.json_path]
  ```
  Update error message: `"Only one of regex, json_schema, ebnf, or json_path can be set."`

---

## Step 3: Handle `json_path` in Grammar Manager

**File**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

In `process_req_with_grammar()`:

- Add `or req.sampling_params.json_path is not None` to the existence check (line 71-75)
- Add `json_path` to the error message string (line 77)
- Add new branch before the `structural_tag` branch (after line 85):
  ```python
  elif req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
      from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_constraint import jsonpath_to_json_schema
      try:
          json_schema_str = jsonpath_to_json_schema(req.sampling_params.json_path)
      except ValueError as e:
          req.set_finish_with_abort(f"Invalid json_path expression: {e}")
          return False
      key = ("json", json_schema_str)
  ```
  This converts to JSON schema and reuses the `("json", ...)` cache key, leveraging existing `dispatch_json()` in all backends.

---

## Step 4: Add `json_path` to Frontend API

**File**: `python/sglang/lang/api.py`

- Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter to `gen()` (after `json_schema`, line 98)
- Pass `json_path` to `SglGen()` constructor (after `json_schema`, line 138)

---

## Step 5: Add `json_path` to IR Layer

**File**: `python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

**`SglSamplingParams` (line 17-138)**:
- Add field: `json_path: Optional[str] = None` (after `json_schema`, line 36)
- Add `"json_path": self.json_path` to `to_srt_kwargs()` return dict (after `json_schema`, line 137)
- Note: `clone()` uses positional args matching only up to `json_schema`; `json_path` follows the same pattern as `dtype`/`regex` (not positionally cloned, but carried via kwargs)

**`SglGen.__init__` (line 451-500)**:
- Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter (after `json_schema`, line 474)
- Pass `json_path=json_path` to `SglSamplingParams()` constructor (after `json_schema=json_schema`, line 499)

---

## Step 6: Add `json_path` to Interpreter

**File**: `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`

- Add `"json_path"` to the field copy list at line 809 (after `"json_schema"`)

---

## Step 7: Add `json_path` to OpenAI-Compatible API Protocol

**File**: `python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

- `$.name` → object with required `name` property
- `$.a.b.c` → nested required object properties
- `$.items[*]` → object with array property
- `$.store.book[*].author` → full nested structure with array
- `$.data[0]` → array with `minItems: 1`
- `$.data[2].name` → array with `minItems: 3`, items have `name`
- Error: missing `$` prefix

## Verification

2. **Import check**: `python -c "from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_constraint import jsonpath_to_json_schema; print(jsonpath_to_json_schema('$.store.book[*].author'))"`

# ==============================================================================
"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

import logging
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union

# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
try:
    import re._parser as sre_parse
except ImportError:
    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SamplingParams:
    """
    The sampling parameters.

    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
    for the documentation.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        top_k: int = -1,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
        n: int = 1,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
        self.stop_strs = stop
        if stop_token_ids:
            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
        else:
            self.stop_token_ids = None
        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.top_p = top_p
        self.top_k = top_k
        self.min_p = min_p
        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
        self.regex = regex
        self.n = n
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
        self.custom_params = custom_params
        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed

        # Process some special cases
        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
            self.temperature = 1.0

        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
                total += MAX_LEN
            else:
                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
            # length
            total += 0
        else:
            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")

            total += MAX_LEN

    return total

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time
from concurrent import futures
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List

import torch

from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
    create_grammar_backend,
)
from sglang.srt.environ import envs

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class GrammarManager:
    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
        self.scheduler = scheduler
        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
                self.server_args,
                scheduler.tokenizer,
                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
            )
        else:
            self.grammar_backend = None

        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank

        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
        )

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.grammar_queue)

    def clear(self):
        if self.grammar_backend:
            self.grammar_backend.reset()

    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0

    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
        for req in self.grammar_queue:
            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
                if req.grammar:
                    req.grammar.cancel()
                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")

    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        # Init grammar cache for this request
        add_to_grammar_queue = False
        if (
            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
        ):
            if self.grammar_backend is None:
                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
            else:
                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)

                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                    key, req.require_reasoning
                )
                req.grammar = value

                if not cache_hit:
                    req.grammar_key = key
                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
                else:
                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        if add_to_grammar_queue:
            self.grammar_queue.append(req)

        return add_to_grammar_queue

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
        """
        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.

        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)

        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
        """
        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()

        # Poll for ready requests
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
                if i in ready_req_idxs:
                    continue

                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
                    continue

                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
                if req.grammar.done():
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)

            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)

        # Check failed requests
        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
                if (
                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
                ):
                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
        else:
            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
                all_gather_output,
                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
            )
            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])

        # Return ready requests
        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)
            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                continue

            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Return failed requests
        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)

            req.grammar.cancel()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
        self.grammar_queue = [
            req
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
        ]
        return return_reqs

choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""

    if choices:
        return SglSelect(
            name,
            choices,
            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
        )

    # check regex is valid
    if regex is not None:
        try:
            re.compile(regex)
        except re.error as e:
            raise e

    return SglGen(
        name,
        max_tokens,
        min_tokens,
        n,
        stop,
        stop_token_ids,
        stop_regex,
        temperature,
        top_p,
        top_k,

class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        max_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        min_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        top_k: Optional[int] = None,
        min_p: Optional[float] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
        super().__init__()
        self.name = name
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
            min_new_tokens=min_new_tokens,
            n=n,
            stop=stop,
            stop_regex=stop_regex,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            temperature=temperature,
            top_p=top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            return_logprob=return_logprob,
            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Gen('{self.name}')"
